mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales signals rising personal consumption and aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positively correlated with RSAFS growth over any 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.5%, labor markets become excessively tight, creating wage inflation fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4.5%, 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with accelerating housing starts signals broad-based economic momentum and supply-side vitality, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in continued expansion.
failure_condition: The joint cross-sectional signal does not produce a positive Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window on 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising Federal Funds rates increase discount rates on equity cash flows and tighten financial conditions, unambiguously predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month regardless of other macro data.
failure_condition: During periods where FEDFUNDS is rising from a low base, 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year calibration window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion of nonfarm payrolls reflects labor demand and income growth that sustains consumption and corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as the trend becomes embedded.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of PAYEMS month-over-month growth on 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, it triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy from the Federal Reserve, which depresses equity prices as discount rates rise, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: When CPI 12-month change exceeds 2.5%, 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising retail sales and low Federal Funds rates creates an accommodative demand environment where consumers borrow and spend freely, amplifying corporate revenue growth and pushing stock prices higher.
failure_condition: The composite interaction signal (RSAFS high & FEDFUNDS low) fails to predict positive 20-day returns with Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During the late-cycle expansion phase, a rise in the unemployment rate of 0.3 percentage points over three months is an early recession signal that triggers defensive positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions (UNRATE < 5.0% and rising), when UNRATE rises 0.3pp over 3 months, 60-day forward returns are not below zero with statistical significance (p > 0.05).
